findings.description: SMTP/email header injection: _render interpolates unvalidated msg.subject and msg.to into CRLF-terminated headers, so an attacker-controlled subject containing '\r\n' can inject arbitrary headers (e.g., Bcc) or split header/body (CWE-93).
Unvalidated msg.to is passed as the sendmail recipient and also rendered into the To: header, allowing envelope/recipient manipulation when the address contains commas or CRLF.
SMTP connection on submission port 587 never calls starttls() and never authenticates, so message bodies are transmitted in cleartext (CWE-319).
